Labs: Hgb 7.5 g/dL, Platelets 45,000/mm³, AST 810 U/L, ALT 420 U/L, Creatinine 1.7 mg/dL, PC <17%.

subParts.q: What is the diagnosis of this case?
choices: Placental abruption with HELLP syndrome and intrauterine fetal death

subParts.q: How to manage this case?
choices: Expectant management with bed rest and close monitoring
Immediate resuscitation (blood products, IV access), correct coagulopathy, proceed with delivery
Urgent MRI to confirm diagnosis before any intervention

subParts.q: What are the possible maternal complications to this case?
choices: DIC, acute renal failure, hepatic failure, pulmonary edema, maternal death
